judge.rationale: The design correctly identifies all three domains (projects, tasks, notifications) and proposes the matching file structure. All port interfaces are properly typed with no `any`, clearly named, and well-documented with ownership annotations. Minor deviations from reference: ProjectLookupPort includes both `projectExists()` and `getProject()` rather than just `exists()` (slightly over-specified but practical), and method naming is more prescriptive (e.g., `getById` vs `get`, `listByProject` vs generic `list(filter)`). These improve clarity without compromising the core FCD principle—surfaces are presented first, and architecture flows from frozen ports.
